title: Constraint–Market Compatibility — NAICS 221115 Wind Electric Power Generation

sections.title: Scope boundary
sections.content:
Mein Solar's scope within 221115 = **onshore park co-location + standalone grid-booster** at the 4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference scale. Offshore wind (turbines 10–15 MW, parks 500+ MW, TSO HV regime) and TSO transmission-scale grid-boosters are **out of scope** and evaluated separately under 221121. Assessment below applies to onshore co-located / park-adjacent BESS.

---

sections.title: Verdict summary
sections.content:
| # | Constraint | Type | Threshold | Verdict | Cost | Time |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity | operational | — | **none** | — | — |
| 2 | Land Footprint and Siting | physical | 40–80 m²/MWh | **none** | — | — |
| 3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | regulatory | per VdS | **none** | — | — |
| 4 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | physical | — | **none** | — | — |
| 5 | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | regulatory | 6–12 months | **mitigable** | low | 0 mo (native) — upgrade for grid-forming |
| 6 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | economic | — | **none** | — | — |
| 7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | environmental | −20 to +50 °C | **none** | — | — |
| 8 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | physical | 10–15% | **none** | — | — |
| 9 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | economic | €250–400/kWh | **none** | — | — |

**Knockouts: 0.** Market **survives**. 1 mitigable (grid-forming firmware + FGW TR3 grid-forming cert for inertia-service revenue, not the base TR8 plant cert).

---

sections.title: Per-constraint rationale
sections.content:
### 1. MV Grid Connection Capacity — none
Wind parks already own or control an MV collector (20/30 kV) and a park substation with dedicated DSO/TSO interconnection. Co-located BESS taps into the existing MV infrastructure — no separate grid-upgrade risk. For retrofit onto operational parks, headroom is usually present because curtailment exists by definition (i.e., the interconnection is undersized for wind peaks, which is exactly why BESS is bought).

### 2. Land Footprint and Siting — none
Wind park sites offer abundant land. 9 MWh × 80 m²/MWh ≈ 720 m² — trivial against park footprints (0.5–1.5 ha per turbine). Container placement adjacent to the substation pad is standard.

### 3. Fire-Protection Clearance — none
Wind parks are rural/remote; distances to buildings and property lines are generous. VdS separation and Feuerwehr access are straightforward. >3 MWh cluster rules apply but are comfortably met.

### 4. Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard — none
Market-agnostic hazard, contained at the product level by NOVEC 1230, compartmentalization, and deflagration venting. Rural siting further reduces third-party exposure.

### 5. Grid Code Certification Lead Time — mitigable
Base VDE-AR-N 4110 + FGW TR8 Type B certification is Mein Solar's native capability (no adaptation effort). **However:** to monetize Wind+BESS's anchor revenue stream (inertia services launching Jan 2026, curtailment-capture via grid-forming dispatch), Mein Solar must add **grid-forming PCS firmware + FGW TR3 grid-forming unit certification** to the stack. This is a targeted capability extension, not a barrier to delivery. Mitigation actions: (1) select PCS with grid-forming firmware option (SMA/Sungrow roadmap), (2) commission TR3 grid-forming unit cert with TÜV/DEKRA, (3) register for inertia-service market prequal. Cost: low (<€10K incremental cert cost per project; PCS firmware is SKU-level choice). Time: parallelizable with standard TR8 dossier — no critical-path impact.

### 6. Battery Supply Chain Concentration — none
Market-agnostic. Cell concentration applies equally across all 221115/221118/531120/etc. Not a 221115-specific barrier.

### 7. Ambient Temperature Envelope — none
German onshore wind sites (coastal and inland) sit comfortably within −20 to +50 °C. Container HVAC manages the internal setpoint. No extreme-climate exposure in Mein Solar's in-scope geography.

### 8. Round-Trip Energy Losses — none
Physics floor applies identically across all markets; does not differentiate 221115 from any other destination. The Wind+BESS revenue stack (€400k/MW/yr) is calculated assuming this loss budget.

### 9. CAPEX Viability Threshold — none
Wind+BESS co-location carries the **strongest €/MW revenue stack of any assessed market** (~€400k/MW/yr — curtailment capture + arbitrage + ancillary services + future inertia). Payback at €250–400/kWh installed is well within the 3–5 year band. Innovation Tender and EEG §50a/§50b mechanisms further improve economics. Business case closes with margin.

---

sections.title: Conservative review (08b two-question test)
sections.content:
Re-testing each potential knockout candidate:

- **No candidate had verdict = knockout**, so no downgrade applied.
- Grid Code Certification (#5) remains **mitigable** (not knockout): FGW TR3 grid-forming cert is a well-defined engineering add, not a physics barrier.
- Scale-gap concern (Mein Solar 4.5 MW ref vs. offshore 500+ MW parks): **scoped out** at market-boundary level per the VN's explicit "fits onshore park-co-location; mismatch with TSO/offshore" finding, not carried as a knockout inside 221115.

---

sections.title: Implications for downstream scoring
sections.content:
- **221115 survives constraint gate** and proceeds to Steps 09–12.
- Single mitigable (grid-forming cert) carries **low cost penalty** into market prioritization.
- Strategic reinforcement: 221115 is HIGH priority (distance 3), the mitigation is a capability investment that also unlocks revenue stream (inertia services) rather than a pure cost — **net value-accretive mitigation**.
- Scale mismatch is handled via scope-limiting (onshore only), not constraint knockout. Offshore/TSO opportunities are redirected to 221121 analysis.

---

9 MWh ≈ 720 m² at the 80 m²/MWh bound — trivial against wind park footprints of 0.5–1.5 ha per turbine. Adjacent-to-substation siting is standard.
Rural park siting provides ample separation to buildings and property lines; VdS clearance and Feuerwehr access easily met even for >3 MWh clusters.
Hazard is market-agnostic and contained at the product level (NOVEC 1230, compartmentalization, deflagration venting). Rural siting further reduces third-party exposure.

Commission FGW TR3 grid-forming unit certification with TÜV or DEKRA
Register asset for BNetzA inertia-service market prequal (launching Jan 2026)

Capability extension, not a delivery barrier. Mitigation is value-accretive (unlocks revenue) rather than a pure cost.
Market-agnostic supply-chain exposure; not differentiating for 221115 vs. any other destination.
German onshore wind sites (coastal and inland) sit within −20 to +50 °C; container HVAC manages internal setpoint. Offshore / extreme-climate exposure is scoped out.
Market-agnostic thermodynamic floor; revenue-stack math at this market already assumes 10–15% loss budget and still yields ~€400k/MW/yr.

Wind+BESS co-location carries the strongest €/MW revenue stack (~€400k/MW/yr: curtailment capture + arbitrage + ancillary + inertia). Payback at €250–400/kWh installed lands in the 3–5 year band with margin. Cost can never be a knockout per component rules.
